evidence_summary: Using pyramid representations can increase robustness to scale variance in object detection.
Attaching the head to the highest-resolution, strongly semantic feature maps can be an alternative to pyramid representations, but may lead to more anchors and increased inference time.
Deep ConvNets can compute a feature hierarchy with an inherent multiscale, pyramidal shape, potentially alleviating the need for explicit image pyramids.
